chunks.text: University of California at Berkeley Department of Electrical Engineering & Computer Sciences Instructional Support Group /share/b/pub/password.help Instructional UNIX Password Service ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

onal UNIX Password Service ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ June 26 2007 CONTENTS: Instructional UNIX Passwords Microsoft Windows Passwords To Request an New Account or Help Instructional UNIX Passwords ---------------------------

ctional UNIX Passwords ---------------------------- The EECS Instructional UNIX password service is called "LDAP". It stores your password and other account information in databases. To change the password, login shell or full name in your

the password, login shell or full name in your UNIX LDAP entry, login (using "ssh") to update.eecs.berkeley.edu. A menu of options will appear. The change propagates to all of the EECS Instructional UNIX computers within a few minutes. F

uctional UNIX computers within a few minutes. For more technical info about LDAP, please type "man ldap" on any of our Solaris systems. The EECS Instructional UNIX passwords only work on the Instructional UNIX computers (please see http:

Instructional UNIX computers (please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/clients for computer names). Non-Instructional UNIX computers in EECS, such as argus.eecs and research computers, use a different password service. Microsoft Windows P

different password service. Microsoft Windows Passwords --------------------------- The Microsoft Windows domain passwords are not synchronized with Instructional UNIX account passwords. You can change your Windows password by pressing the

can change your Windows password by pressing the keys after you have logged onto the account. The EECS Windows domain passwords work on all Windows computers in EECS (Instructional, research and administrative computers). To Request

ch and administrative computers). To Request an New Account or Help --------------------------------- For information about requesting an account on the Instructional computers, please see https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/newusers.html.

tps://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/newusers.html. For help with an account on the Instructional computers, please email inst@eecs.berkeley.edu or visit one of our offices at 378/284/386 Cory and 333 Soda (http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/.p
